    static Map<Ship,Ship3D>
    FleetViewBuilder.buildViews(Board board)

    ShipPlacementController.getShipViews()
     
    private Map<Ship,Ship3D>
    MainGameController.rebuildBoardView(Board board, javafx.scene.Group boardGroup, boolean hideIntactHulls)
    Builds the 3D shape for every ship on an already-placed board and adds it to the scene, then replays every shot already resolved on that board -- a miss becomes an X mark, a hit or sunk cell is applied to the matching ship via Ship3D.markHit(int).

    Methods in com.example.battleship.model.board with parameters of type Ship
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    Board.canPlaceShip(Ship ship)
    Checks whether ship could be placed on this board right now, without actually placing it and without throwing on failure.
    private boolean
    Board.fitsWithinBoard(Ship ship)
    Whether every cell ship would occupy is within the board's 0-9 range, using getWidthInColumns() and getDepthInRows() as the single source of truth for the ship's footprint -- deliberately computed with plain arithmetic rather than by calling getOccupiedPositions(): that method builds a Position for every segment, and Position's own constructor rejects out-of-range coordinates -- exactly the case being checked for here, so it can't be used to check for it.
    void
    BoardListener.onShipPlaced(Ship ship)
    Called right after a ship has been successfully placed on the board.
    void
    BoardListener.onShotResolved(Position position, ShotResult result, Ship ship)
    Called right after a shot has been resolved.
    private boolean
    Board.overlapsExistingShip(Ship ship)
     
    void
    Board.placeShip(Ship ship)
    Places a ship on the board, after validating that it fits within the board and doesn't overlap any ship already placed.
    (package private) void
    Cell.setOccupyingShip(Ship ship)
    Records which ship occupies this cell.
    private void
    Board.validateWithinBounds(Ship ship)

    static Ship
    ShipFactory.createShip(ShipType type, Position origin, Orientation orientation)
    Creates a single ship of the given type at the given position and orientation.
